# Contributing Guidelines

Thank you for contributing to this project.

## Before You Start

1. Search existing issues and pull requests to avoid duplicates.
2. Open an issue first for large changes.
3. Keep changes focused and minimal per pull request.

## Development Workflow

1. Fork and create a branch from main.
2. Use a descriptive branch name, for example: feature/add-julia-example.
3. Make small commits with clear messages.
4. Update tests and docs when behavior changes.

## Commit Style

Prefer concise commit messages such as:

- feat: add package usage docs for npm
- fix: handle already-published package version in workflow
- docs: update release guide

## Pull Request Checklist

- I confirmed tests/build pass locally when applicable.
- I updated documentation for user-facing changes.
- I kept unrelated file changes out of this pull request.
- I linked related issue(s) in the pull request description.

## Language Modules

This repository contains multiple language modules.
When changing physics behavior, keep formulas and outputs aligned across implementations.

## Code of Conduct

By participating, you agree to follow the Code of Conduct in .github/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md.
